Petition For Order Approving Co-Trustees Account (Probate Code Section 17200)
Off Calendar Jun-10-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: David Baer for Donald & Laura Philips. Ronald Shifman for Daniel Philips.

Mr. Baer reported on the sale of the property, that it sold for $6,750,000.00, (this was pursuant to a mediation agreement in which the co-trustee agreed that he would not object to a sale as long as the sale was for an amount in excess of $6,000,000.00) that the agreement was clear regarding the sale, that Daniel signed the agreement as well as signing the deed, that the amount held in reserve should be $600,000.00 ($200,000.00 for each GST trust), that this would be more than ample to cover fees, that the accounting should be approved and that Daniel's request for fees is not appropriate to hold up the accounting.
Mr. Shifman stated that in regards to the sale the petitioners breached the agreement, that the property was not listed, that they just struck a deal with a neighbor, that the beneficiary objected to the sale of the property, that a separate lawsuit is being filed regarding this, that Daniel sent an email requesting information regarding the sale but was refused, that he confirmed his position that the property was undervalued, that in regards to the distribution they want to make sure it is handled properly with an affidavit of death being filed so that there are no problems with title down the road. Mr. Baer stated that his clients had every right to proceed, that it was facilitated by Daniel signing the deed, that there is no basis for allowing this suit to proceed, that the transfer of note is expected to be handled properly and that they will cooperate, and that they request an overruling of the objections to the sale. Mr. Shifman stated that the beneficiary objected to the sale of the residence, that they do not want to be collaterally estopped and that his client is out of pocket for costs and expenses while the other trustees were reimbursed. Mr. Baer responded that the fees reimbursed were for preparation of tax returns and the sale of the home as opposed to litigation fees and that those issues should be reserved as they do not get paid out of the trust. Mr. Shifman stated that his declaration broke out the fees for the various aspects, that he worked on the tax preparation, the accounting matters and the 2 petitions before the court and that all expenses incurred by his client should be reimbursed. Mr. Baer stated that those claims should not be addressed in this accounting petition and that they should be brought in a separate petition. The petition for order approving co-trustees account was taken under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

Petition For Order Approving Co-Trustees Account (Probate Code Section 17200)
Off Calendar May-13-2015 Continued To Jun-08-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Instructing Trustees To Distribute Cash, Recorded Loans, And Tangible Real Proper
Off Calendar Mar-20-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: David Baer for (petitioners) Laura Philips & Donald Philips. Ronald Shifman (via court call) for (respondent) Daniel Philips.

Mr. Baer stated that nothing further has been filed, that Mr. Shifman received the accounting and has disputes but that they are the same disputes he has had throughout the case, that the largest asset was the house that has been sold with the funds being deposited into the account, that the securities have been sold and funds deposited into the account, that there have been minor expenses with the administration of the estate, taxes, etc., that there has been over $4,000,000.00 in the account for over a year and that distribution is being held up. Mr. Shifman stated that he is ready to discuss alternative investments, that he recently received an email from Mr. Baer stating that there is no way to work this out and that the petition ignores the relief he is requesting. Mr. Baer proposed distribution with $600,000.00 to remain in reserve to cover possible legal expenses and additional estate taxes. Mr. Shifman expressed his concern with the amount stating that damages could be as high as $2,000,000.00 and once the assets were distributed there would be no way to recoup. Mr. Baer stated that holding $2,000,000.00 in reserve would be better than reserving the entire trust. The court requested an amended order showing the $2,000,000.00 in reserve and the petition for order instructing trustees to distribute cash, etc., was taken under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of An Accounting From All Three Co-Trustees
Off Calendar Feb-09-2015 Off Calendar

Petition For Order Instructing Trustees To Distribute Cash, Recorded Loans, And Tangible Real Proper
Off Calendar Dec-17-2014 Continued To Mar-16-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: David Baer appeared by telephone for Laura Philips and Donald Philips. Ronald Shifman appeared by telephone for Daniel Philips (Co-Trustee).

Mr. Baer stated that mediation had occurred three different times, that he did not think another mediation would be appropriate at this time, that there is no petition for an accounting and that all three parties are co-trustees. Counsel also discussed the issue of specific information having not been requested, the need to look at various invoices to see what has been paid, nothing specific having been raised to justify continuing to hold distribution of the $4,000,000.00, the co-trustees having bank statements and tax records, the claim that the real property was sold for under market value, the request for the $600,000.00 reserve, etc. The Court ordered an accounting from all three co-trustees and that all trustees cooperate in preparation of the accounting. Status on filing of the accounting was set for February 9, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Petition for Order Instructing Trustees to Distribute Cash, etc. was continued to March 16, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)
